title: arenaria procera subsp. polaris
intro:
Arenaria procera subsp. polaris, also known as Arctic Sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, characterized by its small white flowers and ability to thrive in arctic environments.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Arctic Sandwort include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various health conditions.

In herbal preparations, Arctic Sandwort is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is not extensively documented and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety. While some studies suggest its potential benefits, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
